channel.title: Defense
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: 4 crew members eject safely after 2 Navy jets crash during air show in Idaho
item.description: “Everyone is safe and I think that’s the most important thing,” a spokespersons said.

media:content.media:credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: A U.S. Navy EA18-G Growler is seen Dec. 17, 2025, over Ceiba, Puerto Rico.

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press
item.dc:contributor: By Associated Press

item.title: USS Ford returns home after 11-month deployment supporting the Iran war and Maduro’s capture
item.description: The warship and two destroyers docked in Norfolk, Virginia, with about 5,000 sailors waiting to see their families for the first time since June.

media:content.media:title: A sailor is greeted after disembarking aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford at Naval Station Norfolk on May 16, 2026, in Norfolk, Virginia. The carrier returned home after an 11-month deployment, the longest since the Vietnam War. (Photo by Mike Kropf/Getty Images)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press
item.dc:contributor: By Associated Press

item.title: ‘Slap in the face': Republicans skewer Pentagon over Poland move
item.description: Army leaders also appeared taken aback by the administration’s abrupt action.

media:content.media:title: Army Secretary Daniel Driscoll and acting Army Chief of Staff Christopher LaNeve prepare to testify before the House Armed Services Committee on May 15, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: ‘20 years is enough’: Trump puts a timeline on limiting Iran’s nuclear program
item.description: The allowance for Iran to enrich uranium at any point in the future — even decades out — marks a shift for the president.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump said he isn’t ready to accept a deal with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Alex Gangitano
item.dc:contributor: By Alex Gangitano

item.title: Pentagon officials stunned by Hegseth decision on troops in Poland
item.description: It wasn’t clear why the Defense secretary issued the order not to send troops on a routine mission to a country the administration refers to as a “model ally.”

media:content.media:title: Soldiers of the U.S. Army 2nd Cavalry Regiment Stryker Brigade watch Apache helicopters engage during NATO military exercises near Bemowo Piskie, Poland, on May 7, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch

item.title: Pentagon official: Iran still threatens Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tehran’s ability to scare off commercial ships continues to give it leverage in any peace negotiation with the U.S.

media:content.media:title: Adm. Brad Cooper, commander of U.S. Central Command, testifies before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Capitol Hill, on May 14, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III

item.title: Trump’s disappearing China hawks
item.description: The once vocal GOP wing that warned against making deals with Beijing has largely left the conversation ahead of Trump’s China summit.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump walks with China's Vice President Han Zheng during a welcome ceremony on May 13, 2026, at Beijing Capital International Airport.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Katherine Long, Paul McLeary and Ari Hawkins
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Katherine Long, Paul McLeary and Ari Hawkins

item.title: Pentagon's estimate for Iran war grows to $29B
item.description: The latest increase comes even as the U.S. says the fighting has largely stopped.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chair of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Caine prepare to testify before the House Appropriations Subcommittee on Defense on Capitol Hill in Washington, on May 12, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: German lawmaker downplays Trump troop withdrawal
item.description: German lawmaker Roderich Kiesewetter acknowledged tense relations between Berlin and Washington but argued German leaders could have done a better job of calibrating their criticisms of the Iran war.

media:content.media:description: German lawmaker downplays Trump troop withdrawal
media:content.media:title: German lawmaker downplays Trump troop withdrawal

item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Slotkin leaves door open to 2028 bid
item.description: The Michigan Democrat and prominent national security voice said the country is ready for a “new generation” of candidates.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Slotkin on 2028 presidential race

item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump’s Beijing trip should make Taiwan ‘nervous,’ Slotkin says
item.description: The Senate Armed Services member and former Pentagon official warned the president could easily fall into a trap due to his eagerness to strike a deal with China.

media:content.media:description: Sen. Slotkin talks Taiwan ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
media:content.media:title: Sen. Slotkin talks Taiwan ahead of Trump-Xi Summit

item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Top Republican wants more details on Iran war if ceasefire ends
item.description: As the war's future seems uncertain, there are signs GOP patience is starting to fray.

media:content.media:description: Top Republican wants more details on Iran war if ceasefire ends
media:content.media:title: Top Republican wants more details on Iran war if ceasefire ends

item.dc:creator: By Audrey Decker
item.dc:contributor: By Audrey Decker

item.title: What Beijing has learned about the US from the Iran war
item.description: Current and former U.S. defense officials worry headwinds from the conflict with Tehran leave Beijing with the upper hand.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump, left, and Chinese President Xi Jinping arrive for talks at the Gimhae Air Base in Busan, South Korea, on Oct. 30, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary

item.title: Kelly’s profile rises as he heads toward another likely victory over Hegseth
item.description: The saga has turned the Democratic senator into a high-profile opponent of the administration and elevated his national standing ahead of a possible presidential run.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Mark Kelly speaks during a press conference at the U.S. Capitol on Dec. 1, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III, Connor O'Brien and Lisa Kashinsky
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III, Connor O'Brien and Lisa Kashinsky

item.title: Hegseth says battle for Hormuz is ‘separate and distinct' from the Iran war
item.description: The Defense secretary distanced Project Freedom, the latest effort to reopen the strait, from the rest of the Iran war.

media:content.media:title: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth takes questions during a press briefing at the Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia, on May 5, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch

item.title: US and Iranian boat attacks threaten ceasefire
item.description: The exchange occurred a day after President Donald Trump announced the Navy would help commercial vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz.

media:content.media:title: A patrol boat moves through the water as cargo ships sit in the Strait of Hormuz off Bandar Abbas, Iran on May 2, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary, Jack Detsch and Cheyanne M. Daniels
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary, Jack Detsch and Cheyanne M. Daniels

item.title: Germany’s Pistorius plays down US troop cut, stresses Europe’s defense push
item.description: “If we are to remain transatlantic, we must strengthen the European pillar within NATO,” German defense minister said in response to planned withdrawal of American troops from his country.

media:content.media:title: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius gives a statement to the assembled media during a visit of the Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems (TKMS) submarine shipyard on April 22, 2026, in Kiel, Germany.

item.dc:creator: By Chris Lunday and Rory O’Neill
item.dc:contributor: By Chris Lunday and Rory O’Neill

item.title: Hegseth orders withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany
item.description: The drawdown, which comes after the country’s leader sparred with President Donald Trump over the Iran war, leaves about 33,000 troops there.

media:content.media:title: Members of the U.S. Army 3rd Brigade, 1st Cavalry Division, participate in the "Combined Resolve" military exercise at the U.S. Army 7th Army Training Command Joint Multinational Readiness Center in Hohenfels, Germany, on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Aaron Pellish and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Aaron Pellish and Paul McLeary

item.title: Trump tells Congress the Iran war has ‘terminated’ as legal deadline hits
item.description: The letter to lawmakers attempts to justify why the president is not seeking congressional authorization after the conflict reaches a 60-day threshold.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump speaks in the Oval Office on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Eli Stokols
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Eli Stokols

item.title: Hegseth says Iran war can continue despite legal deadline
item.description: The Pentagon chief told senators a ceasefire in the Middle East pauses a 60-day war powers clock.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, left, and Chair of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Caine testify before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Capitol Hill on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Trump’s call to reduce US troops in Germany shocks Pentagon
item.description: The Defense Department "was not expecting it," a congressional aide said.

media:content.media:title: U.S. Army troops simulate an attack against an enemy during a demonstration of capabilities at the Grafenwoehr training grounds on March 13, 2026, near Grafenwoehr, Germany.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary and Stefanie Bolzen
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary and Stefanie Bolzen

item.title: Trump says US considering reducing troops in Germany
item.description: There are 38,000 U.S. troops and personnel stationed in Germany, and cutting forces there would face opposition on Capitol Hill.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump speaks during a meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, March 3, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Aaron Pellish
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Aaron Pellish

item.title: As Iran war drags on, Hegseth labels Dems obstacle to peace
item.description: His decision to label critics as short-sighted and unpatriotic pulls a page from the Donald Trump playbook: when threatened, attack.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testifies before the House Armed Services Committee on Capitol Hill in Washington, on April 29, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Pentagon official: Iran war has cost $25B
item.description: The figure is the most specific price tag the administration has provided on the U.S. military conflict.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Dan Caine testify before the House Armed Services Committee on April 29, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III

item.title: Senate Armed Services chair: Trump should resume strikes on Iran
item.description: Sen. Roger Wicker said the administration needs to “finish destroying Iran’s conventional military capabilities.”

media:content.media:title: Sen. Roger Wicker, R-Miss., the Senate Armed Services Committee chair, leaves a closed-door briefing on the Iran War at the Capitol on March 10, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III

item.title: Hegseth declares victory while preparing for more war with Iran
item.description: The administration is still sending more forces to the region — and asking for help from allies — even as the White House says the conflict is over.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks to members of the media during a press briefing at the Pentagon on April 16, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Golden Dome dreams face harsh budget reality
item.description: Congressional Republicans are increasingly reluctant to move ahead with a partisan budget bill that was supposed to cover the program’s costs.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump and Rep. Jim Banks (R-Ind.) listen as Space Force Gen. Michael Guetlein speaks in the Oval Office about the Golden Dome missile defense system May 20, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Audrey Decker and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Audrey Decker and Paul McLeary

item.title: Acting Navy secretary, once a political lightning rod, finds surprising support
item.description: Hung Cao, known as a partisan firebrand, has built credibility inside the Pentagon.

media:content.media:title: Then-Republican Senate candidate Hung Cao speaks during a rally for President Donald Trump on June 28, 2024, in Chesapeake, Virginia.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Dasha Burns, Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Dasha Burns, Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Senate again declines to curb Trump on Iran amid uncertain ceasefire, peace talks
item.description: It's the fifth time a Democratic-led effort to rein in Trump has failed, although Republicans are eyeing an approaching war powers deadline.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.), Sen. Tim Kaine (D-Va.), Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.), and Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) speak with veterans about the Iran war at Capitol Hill on March 18, 2026 in Washington, D.C.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Navy secretary is out amid Pentagon infighting
item.description: John Phelan sparked tensions within the department over his support for a new battleship.

media:content.media:title: John Phelan, who served just over a year in his post, had helped conceive of the new battleships to curry favor with President Donald Trump, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary, Daniel Lippman and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary, Daniel Lippman and Connor O'Brien
